chapter_title.ar: باب أَكْلِ مَا يَسْقُطُ مِنَ الْخِوَانِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – Eat what falls from the food-spread

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/118)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/118)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَنْ أَكَلَ فِي مَنْزِلِهِ طَعَاماً فَسَقَطَ مِنْهُ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَلْيَتَنَاوَلْهُ وَ مَنْ أَكَلَ فِي الصَّحْرَاءِ أَوْ خَارِجاً فَلْيَتْرُكْهُ لِلطَّيْرِ وَ السَّبُعِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Moamar Bin Khallad who said,

‘I heard Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The one who eats food in his house, so something from it falls off, so let him take it; and the one who eats in the desert, or outside, so let him leave it for the birds and the wild animals’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Mu’ammar ibn Khallad who has said the following: “I once heard abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘If one eats food in his house and pieces of food fall off, he must pick them up and eat; but if one eats food in the wilderness or outdoors he should leave such pieces for the birds and beasts.”’ (See also Ch. 48, h 15)
